q:
play / football
 The children came into the house. They had a football and they were both very tired. They ___.

look forward to / it
 I was disappointed when I had to cancel my holiday. I ___.
a: I had been looking forward to it.

q:
have / a bad dream
 Anna woke up in the middle of the night. She was frightened and didn't know where she was. She ___.
a: She had been having a bad dream.

q:
watch / a film
 When I got home, Mark was sitting in front of the TV. He had just turned it off. He ___.
a: He had been watching a film.

q:
wait / a long time
 The people waiting at the bus stop were getting impatient. The bus was very late. They ___.
a: They had been waiting a long time.

q:
wait / be
 I had arranged to meet Tom in a restaurant. I realized that I ___ for 20 minutes when I ___ in the wrong restaurant.
a: I realized that I had been waiting for 20 minutes when I was in the wrong restaurant.

q:
go / work
 Sarah worked in a company for a long time. At the time the company ___ out of business, Sarah ___ there for twelve years.
a: At the time the company went out of business, Sarah had been working there for twelve years.

q:
play / start
 The orchestra ___ for about ten minutes when a man in the audience suddenly ___ shouting.
a: The orchestra had been playing for about ten minutes when a man in the audience suddenly started shouting.

q:
travel
 At the end of our journey we were extremely tired. We ___ for more than 24 hours.
a: We had been travelling for more than 24 hours.

q:
look
 James was on his hands and knees on the floor. He ___ for his contact lens.
a: He was looking for his contact lens.

q:
run
 Sue was sitting on the ground. She was out of breath. She ___.

walk
 John and I went for a walk. He ___ very fast and I had difficulty keeping up with him.
a: He was walking very fast and I had difficulty keeping up with him.

q:
have
 I was sad when I sold my car. I ___ it for a very long time.
a: I had had it for a very long time.

q:
go
 I was sad when my local cafe closed. I ___ there for many years.
a: I had been going there for many years.

q:
wait
 I had arranged to meet Kate, but I was late. When I finally arrived, she ___ for me.
a: When I finally arrived, she was waiting for me.

q:
wait
 Kate was annoyed when I arrived because she ___ such a long time.
a: Kate was annoyed when I arrived because she had been waiting such a long time.

q:
work
 When I started a few years ago, Joe ___ already ___ there.
a: When I started a few years ago, Joe was already working there.

q:
work
 At the time I started, Joe had already ___ there for two years.
a: At the time I started, Joe had already been working there for two years.

q:
work
 Joe still works for the company. He ___ there a long time now.
a: He has been working there a long time now.
